name: Freezing Dough
recipeInstructions:
1. Let dough rise until doubled in size (approximately 20 minutes at 250°F).
2. Do not brown the rolls; let cool slightly on top.
3. Remove from pan and allow to cool completely on a rack.
4. Store up to 7 days in the refrigerator or 3 months in the freezer.
5. Wrap tightly in foil, then place in a plastic bag.
6. To bake, thaw at room temperature for several hours (or overnight).
7. Bake in an oven set to 350°F for 8 minutes until golden brown.
This method is ideal for meal preparation ahead of time for holidays like Easter, Thanksgiving, or Christmas, or any large gathering. I froze cinnamon rolls without additional rising time after rolling them out and slicing as usual before placing them into 9-inch pie pans. The next morning, I placed one pan on the counter to thaw and baked in the oven; they turned out just as good as if made traditionally. Dinner rolls would also work similarly with proper thawing time.
